tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required ŌĆö omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) ŌĆö it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":57980,"title":"[MySql] Docs: Complete README for new Azure.Provisioning.MySql package","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` **Service directory:** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` **Triggered by:** PR #57773 by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed PR #57773 introduced the brand-new `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` package (commit `[REDACTED]`), providing declarative provisioning for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers. The package includes 10+ resource types: `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`, `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`, `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`, `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`, `AdvancedThreatProtection`, and more. ## Gaps Found - **"Authenticate the Client" section is empty** ŌĆö the Getting Started section has a heading but no content explaining how to authenticate - **"Key concepts" section is skeletal** ŌĆö only one generic sentence; none of the key resource types (`MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, etc.) are listed or explained - **"Examples" section is completely empty** ŌĆö no code samples showing how to provision a MySQL flexible server, create a database, or configure a firewall rule - **"Next steps" section is completely empty** ŌĆö no links to Azure documentation, related packages, or samples - **No sample test files exist** ŌĆö `tests/Samples/` is empty so there are no snippet-backed examples to auto-generate ­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` Replace the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section with: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` is a provisioning library ŌĆö it generates Bicep/ARM templates for deployment via `azd` or the Azure CLI. No runtime credentials are required to define infrastructure. Authentication is only needed when deploying the generated templates, which is handled by the [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` Replace the skeletal `## Key concepts` section with: ```markdown ## Key concepts `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` allows you to define Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server infrastructure declaratively in .NET. The library generates Bicep templates that can be deployed using `azd` or the Azure CLI. Key resource types available in this library: | Resource Type | Description | |---|---| | `MySqlFlexibleServer` | The primary MySQL Flexible Server resource | | `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` | A database hosted on the flexible server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` | IP firewall rules for the server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration` | Server parameter configurations | | `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator` | Azure Active Directory administrator for the server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` | Backup resources for the server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance` | Scheduled maintenance windows | | `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection` | Private endpoint connections | | `AdvancedThreatProtection` | Advanced threat protection settings | For a full conceptual overview of the provisioning library pattern, see the [Azure.Provisioning README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md). ``` Replace the empty `## Examples` section with a snippet placeholder: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a MySQL Flexible Server ``` ``` ``` (The actual code content will be injected by `Update-Snippets.ps1` once the sample test file is created in Step 2.) Replace the empty `## Next steps` section with: ```markdown ## Next steps - [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure Developer CLI (azd) documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure.Provisioning library README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) - [NuGet package]((www.nuget.org/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **Create** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample01_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.Provisioning; using Azure.Provisioning.MySql; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests.Samples; public class Sample01_MySqlFlexibleServer { [Test] [IgnoreOnCoverage] public void CreateMySqlFlexibleServer() { #region Snippet:Sample_MySqlFlexibleServer // Create a new infrastructure definition var infrastructure = new Infrastructure("mysqlDemo"); // Define a MySQL Flexible Server var server = new MySqlFlexibleServer("mysqlserver") { Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku { Name = "Standard_D2ds_v4", Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose }, AdministratorLogin = "adminUser", AdministratorLoginPassword = new BicepSecretValue("adminPassword"), Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021, Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage { StorageSizeInGB = 20 }, Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties { BackupRetentionDays = 7, GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled } }; infrastructure.Add(server); // Add a database to the server var database = new MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase("mydb") { Parent = server, Charset = "utf8mb4", Collation = "utf8mb4_unicode_ci" }; infrastructure.Add(database); // Add a firewall rule to allow Azure services var firewallRule = new MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule("allowAzureServices") { Parent = server, StartIPAddress = "0.0.0.0", EndIPAddress = "0.0.0.0" }; infrastructure.Add(firewallRule); #endregion } } ``` **README location:** Insert the snippet placeholder under `## Examples` / `### Create a MySQL Flexible Server`. **SnippetName:** `Sample_MySqlFlexibleServer` ### Step 3: Verify README structure All required sections exist in `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` but the following are missing content: 1. Ō£ģ Getting started / Install the package ŌĆö present 2. Ō£ģ Getting started / Prerequisites ŌĆö present 3. ŌØī Getting started / Authenticate the client ŌĆö **empty, needs content** (see Step 1) 4. ŌØī Key concepts ŌĆö **skeleton only, needs expansion** (see Step 1) 5. ŌØī Examples ŌĆö **empty, needs snippet placeholder** (see Steps 1 and 2) 6. Ō£ģ Troubleshooting ŌĆö present 7. ŌØī Next steps ŌĆö **empty, needs links** (see Step 1) 8. Ō£ģ Contributing ŌĆö present Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` ŌĆö injects snippet code from the test file into README.md placeholders; verify README was updated with `git diff` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` ŌĆö regenerates API listing files if any public types were changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` ŌĆö ensures consistent formatting ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24175460057/agentic_workflow) ┬Ę ŌŚÅ 560.1K ┬Ę [ŌŚĘ](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57980","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T06:27:36Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T06:27:36Z"}

{"number":57980,"title":"[MySql] Docs: Complete README for new Azure.Provisioning.MySql package","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` **Service directory:** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` **Triggered by:** PR #57773 by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed PR #57773 introduced the brand-new `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` package (commit `[REDACTED]`), providing declarative provisioning for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers. The package includes 10+ resource types: `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`, `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`, `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`, `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`, `AdvancedThreatProtection`, and more. ## Gaps Found - **"Authenticate the Client" section is empty** ŌĆö the Getting Started section has a heading but no content explaining how to authenticate - **"Key concepts" section is skeletal** ŌĆö only one generic sentence; none of the key resource types (`MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, etc.) are listed or explained - **"Examples" section is completely empty** ŌĆö no code samples showing how to provision a MySQL flexible server, create a database, or configure a firewall rule - **"Next steps" section is completely empty** ŌĆö no links to Azure documentation, related packages, or samples - **No sample test files exist** ŌĆö `tests/Samples/` is empty so there are no snippet-backed examples to auto-generate ­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` Replace the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section with: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` is a provisioning library ŌĆö it generates Bicep/ARM templates for deployment via `azd` or the Azure CLI. No runtime credentials are required to define infrastructure. Authentication is only needed when deploying the generated templates, which is handled by the [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` Replace the skeletal `## Key concepts` section with: ```markdown ## Key concepts `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` allows you to define Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server infrastructure declaratively in .NET. The library generates Bicep templates that can be deployed using `azd` or the Azure CLI. Key resource types available in this library: | Resource Type | Description | |---|---| | `MySqlFlexibleServer` | The primary MySQL Flexible Server resource | | `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` | A database hosted on the flexible server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` | IP firewall rules for the server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration` | Server parameter configurations | | `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator` | Azure Active Directory administrator for the server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` | Backup resources for the server | | `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance` | Scheduled maintenance windows | | `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection` | Private endpoint connections | | `AdvancedThreatProtection` | Advanced threat protection settings | For a full conceptual overview of the provisioning library pattern, see the [Azure.Provisioning README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md). ``` Replace the empty `## Examples` section with a snippet placeholder: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a MySQL Flexible Server ``` ``` ``` (The actual code content will be injected by `Update-Snippets.ps1` once the sample test file is created in Step 2.) Replace the empty `## Next steps` section with: ```markdown ## Next steps - [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure Developer CLI (azd) documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure.Provisioning library README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) - [NuGet package]((www.nuget.org/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **Create** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample01_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.Provisioning; using Azure.Provisioning.MySql; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests.Samples; public class Sample01_MySqlFlexibleServer { [Test] [IgnoreOnCoverage] public void CreateMySqlFlexibleServer() { #region Snippet:Sample_MySqlFlexibleServer // Create a new infrastructure definition var infrastructure = new Infrastructure("mysqlDemo"); // Define a MySQL Flexible Server var server = new MySqlFlexibleServer("mysqlserver") { Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku { Name = "Standard_D2ds_v4", Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose }, AdministratorLogin = "adminUser", AdministratorLoginPassword = new BicepSecretValue("adminPassword"), Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021, Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage { StorageSizeInGB = 20 }, Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties { BackupRetentionDays = 7, GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled } }; infrastructure.Add(server); // Add a database to the server var database = new MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase("mydb") { Parent = server, Charset = "utf8mb4", Collation = "utf8mb4_unicode_ci" }; infrastructure.Add(database); // Add a firewall rule to allow Azure services var firewallRule = new MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule("allowAzureServices") { Parent = server, StartIPAddress = "0.0.0.0", EndIPAddress = "0.0.0.0" }; infrastructure.Add(firewallRule); #endregion } } ``` **README location:** Insert the snippet placeholder under `## Examples` / `### Create a MySQL Flexible Server`. **SnippetName:** `Sample_MySqlFlexibleServer` ### Step 3: Verify README structure All required sections exist in `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` but the following are missing content: 1. Ō£ģ Getting started / Install the package ŌĆö present 2. Ō£ģ Getting started / Prerequisites ŌĆö present 3. ŌØī Getting started / Authenticate the client ŌĆö **empty, needs content** (see Step 1) 4. ŌØī Key concepts ŌĆö **skeleton only, needs expansion** (see Step 1) 5. ŌØī Examples ŌĆö **empty, needs snippet placeholder** (see Steps 1 and 2) 6. Ō£ģ Troubleshooting ŌĆö present 7. ŌØī Next steps ŌĆö **empty, needs links** (see Step 1) 8. Ō£ģ Contributing ŌĆö present Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` ŌĆö injects snippet code from the test file into README.md placeholders; verify README was updated with `git diff` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` ŌĆö regenerates API listing files if any public types were changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` ŌĆö ensures consistent formatting ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24175460057/agentic_workflow) ┬Ę ŌŚÅ 560.1K ┬Ę [ŌŚĘ](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57980","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T06:27:36Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T06:27:36Z"}

content.text:
{"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4229883581,"number":57979,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[MySql Provisioning] Docs: Add examples and complete README for Azure.Provisioning.MySql","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` **Service directory:** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` **Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (PR #57773) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed Introduced a new `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` package (1.0.0-beta.1) using the new provisioning generator. The package adds declarative provisioning support for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers, including these resource types: - `MySqlFlexibleServer` - `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` - `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` - `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration` - `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator` - `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` / `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2` - `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance` - `MySqlFlexibleServersCapability` - `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection` - `AdvancedThreatProtection` ## Gaps Found - **`Authenticate the Client` section is empty** ŌĆö no guidance on how to authenticate when using the provisioning library - **`Examples` section is empty** ŌĆö no code snippets showing how to create a MySQL Flexible Server, add a firewall rule, configure a database, etc. - **`Next steps` section is empty** ŌĆö no links to related docs, samples, or the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) workflow - **No sample files exist** ŌĆö `tests/Samples/` directory does not exist; there are no test-backed snippets for snippet extraction
­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md`** Replace the empty sections with content as follows: 1. Fill `### Authenticate the Client` with a note that no explicit authentication is needed ŌĆö the provisioning library generates Bicep/ARM templates; authentication occurs when deploying via `azd` or the Azure CLI. 2. Fill `## Examples` with at least two snippet placeholders: one for creating a basic MySQL Flexible Server and one for adding a firewall rule. 3. Fill `## Next steps` with links to: - [Azure.Provisioning overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) - Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) getting started - Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server documentation Example README diff for the `## Examples` section: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a MySQL Flexible Server ```C# Snippet:MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer Infrastructure infra = new(); MySqlFlexibleServer server = new("mysqlServer", MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30) { Location = AzureLocation.EastUS, Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku { Name = "Standard_D4ds_v4", Tier = ServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose, }, }; infra.Add(server); ``` ### Add a Firewall Rule ```C# Snippet:MySqlProvisioningAddFirewallRule MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule firewallRule = new("allowAll") { StartIpAddress = "0.0.0.0", EndIpAddress = "255.255.255.255", Parent = server, }; infra.Add(firewallRule); ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets 1. **Create the sample test file** at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlProvisioning.cs` Use `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets` samples as the canonical example for test file structure. The file should: - Wrap examples in `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` - Use `[Test]` (NUnit) attribute on each method - Wrap test-only teardown/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif` Suggested snippets: - `MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer` ŌĆö demonstrates creating a `MySqlFlexibleServer` - `MySqlProvisioningAddFirewallRule` ŌĆö demonstrates adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` as a child resource - `MySqlProvisioningAddDatabase` ŌĆö demonstrates adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` Reference an existing provisioning sample, e.g. `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/tests/Samples/`, for the test class shape. 2. **Add snippet placeholders in `README.md`** under `## Examples` in the exact format: ```` ```C# Snippet:MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` must contain these sections. Currently missing/incomplete sections: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started ŌåÆ Install the package | Ō£ģ Present | | Getting started ŌåÆ Prerequisites | Ō£ģ Present | | Getting started ŌåÆ Authenticate the Client | ŌØī Empty | | Key concepts | Ō£ģ Present (brief) | | Examples | ŌØī Empty | | Troubleshooting | Ō£ģ Present | | Next steps | ŌØī Empty | | Contributing | Ō£ģ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. For the **Authenticate the Client** section, adapt the pattern from other provisioning packages (e.g. `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/README.md`): note that this library generates infrastructure-as-code (Bicep) and authentication is handled at deployment time via `azd` or ARM. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` ŌĆö injects snippet code from test files into README placeholders; verify the README diff looks correct 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` ŌĆö regenerates public API listing files (only needed if public types changed) 5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## ­¤Ä» Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue documents gaps in the README for the newly introduced `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` package (added via PR #57773). The "Authenticate the Client", "Examples", and "Next steps" sections are empty and need to be completed with relevant content and code snippets.

<details>
<summary>­¤ōŗ Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` (new package, 1.0.0-beta.1)
**Affected API:** README documentation; `tests/Samples/` directory
**Scenarios:**
- "Authenticate the Client" section is empty ŌĆö no guidance on auth for provisioning libraries
- "Key concepts" section is skeletal ŌĆö key resource types not listed or described
- "Examples" section is completely empty ŌĆö no code samples for server provisioning, database creation, or firewall rules
- "Next steps" section is completely empty ŌĆö no links to Azure docs, azd CLI, or related packages
- No sample test files exist in `tests/Samples/` for snippet extraction

**Root ask:** Fill in the missing README sections and create a sample test file (`tests/Samples/Sample01_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs`) so the provisioning library is properly documented and usable by customers.
</details>

<details>
<summary>­¤öÄ Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue; no runtime bug is reported. The issue was auto-generated by the "Update Docs" workflow when PR #57773 introduced the new package without complete documentation.

A nearly identical issue (#57979) was filed moments earlier for the same package by the same bot workflow ŌĆö this may be a duplicate worth reviewing.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` to confirm the empty sections
2. Check `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/tests/Samples/` for a reference sample test structure
3. Create `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample01_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs` with `#region Snippet:` markers
4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` to inject snippets into README placeholders
5. Run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test` to validate the new sample compiles and passes
</details>

<details>
<summary>­¤ÅĘ’ĖÅ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Provisioning` ŌĆö the package name `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` explicitly starts with `Azure.Provisioning`, matching the Provisioning category rule
- **Service:** `MySQL` ŌĆö the package targets Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server; the service label "MySQL" (color #e99695) was confirmed by the similar issue #57979
- **Confidence:** High ŌĆö the issue clearly names the package, service directory, and category; confirmed by reference issue #57979 with the same label combination
</details>

<details>
<summary>­¤æź Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %MySQL` (line 776) ŌĆö bottom-to-top scan; "MySQL" is the first matching entry encountered; ALL labels in the entry ("MySQL") are present in the issue's predicted labels
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ajlam @ambhatna @kummanish
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; mentioned ServiceOwners via routing comment; issue left unassigned (ServiceOwners path, no AzureSdkOwners); "needs-team-attention" not added since issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported
- **Scan notes:** Entries from line 1228 upward were checked ŌĆö all management-specific entries (e.g., `%SQL %Mgmt`, `%Service Fabric %Mgmt`) required "Mgmt" which is not in the predicted labels; `%Provisioning` at line 415 would also match but was not reached since `%MySQL` at line 776 was the first match scanning bottom-to-top
</details>
